info.title: CipherSwarm Agent API
info.description: The CipherSwarm Agent API is used to allow agents to connect to the CipherSwarm server.

bearer_auth.description: Bearer token authentication using agent API tokens

ErrorObject.description: Standard error response returned by all API endpoints

Agent.description: A cracking agent registered with CipherSwarm

operating_system.description: The operating system of the agent

items.description: The descriptive name of a GPU or CPU device.

AdvancedAgentConfiguration.description: Advanced hashcat and agent configuration options

agent_update_interval.description: The interval in seconds to check for agent updates

use_native_hashcat.description: Use the hashcat binary already installed on the client system

backend_device.description: The device to use for hashcat, separated by commas

opencl_devices.description: The OpenCL device types to use for hashcat, separated by commas

enable_additional_hash_types.description: Causes hashcat to perform benchmark-all, rather than just benchmark

HashcatBenchmark.description: A single hashcat benchmark result for a specific hash type and device

runtime.description: The runtime of the benchmark in milliseconds.

hash_speed.description: The speed of the benchmark in hashes per second.

device.description: The device used for the benchmark

Attack.description: A hashcat attack configuration assigned to an agent

increment_minimum.description: The start of the increment range

increment_maximum.description: The end of the increment range

left_rule.description: The left-hand rule for combinator attacks

right_rule.description: The right-hand rule for combinator attacks

custom_charset_1.description: Custom charset 1 for hashcat mask attacks

custom_charset_2.description: Custom charset 2 for hashcat mask attacks

custom_charset_3.description: Custom charset 3 for hashcat mask attacks

custom_charset_4.description: Custom charset 4 for hashcat mask attacks

hash_list_id.description: The id of the hash list

hash_list_url.description: The download URL for the hash list

hash_list_checksum.description: The MD5 checksum of the hash list

HashcatResult.description: A cracked hash result submitted by an agent

timestamp.description: The time the hash was cracked

Task.description: A unit of work assigned to an agent for a specific attack

start_date.description: The time the task was started

AttackResourceFile.description: A downloadable resource file (word list, rule list, or mask list) used by an attack

id.description: The id of the resource file

download_url.description: The download URL of the resource file

checksum.description: The MD5 checksum of the resource file

file_name.description: The name of the resource file

TaskStatus.description: A hashcat status update submitted by an agent during task execution

time.description: The time the status was received

restore_point.description: The restore point of the task

device_statuses.description: The status of the devices used for the task

DeviceStatus.description: Status and performance metrics for a single GPU or CPU device

temperature.description: The temperature of the device, or -1 if unmonitored.

HashcatGuess.description: Current hashcat guess progress including base and modifier values

guess_base.description: The base value used for the guess (for example, the mask)

guess_base_count.description: The number of times the base value was used

guess_base_offset.description: The offset of the base value

guess_base_percentage.description: The percentage completion of the base value

guess_mod.description: The modifier used for the guess (for example, the wordlist)

guess_mod_count.description: The number of times the modifier was used

guess_mod_percentage.description: The percentage completion of the modifier

guess_mode.description: The mode used for the guess

get.summary: Gets an instance of an agent

operating_system.description: The operating system of the agent

items.description: The descriptive name of a GPU or CPU device.

post.summary: Send a heartbeat for an agent
post.description: Send a heartbeat for an agent to keep it alive. Optionally accepts an 'activity' parameter in the request body to track the agent's current activity state.

schema.description: The response to an agent heartbeat

state.description:
The state of the agent:
                       * `pending` - The agent needs to perform the setup process again.
                       * `active` - The agent is ready to accept tasks, all is good.
                       * `error` - The agent has encountered an error and needs to be checked.
                       * `stopped` - The agent has been stopped by the user.
                       * `offline` - The agent has not checked in recently and is considered offline.

activity.description: Current agent activity state. Known values: starting, benchmarking, updating, downloading, waiting, cracking, stopping. Future versions may support additional values.

post.description: Submit a benchmark for an agent

schema.description: Hashcat benchmark results for the agent

post.summary: Submit an error for an agent
post.description: Submit an error for an agent

severity.description:
The severity of the error:
                       * `info` - Informational message, no action required.
                       * `warning` - Non-critical error, no action required. Anticipated, but not necessarily problematic.
                       * `minor` - Minor error, no action required. Should be investigated, but the task can continue.
                       * `major` - Major error, action required. The task should be investigated and possibly restarted.
                       * `critical` - Critical error, action required. The task should be stopped and investigated.
                        * `fatal` - Fatal error, action required. The agent cannot continue with the task and should not be reattempted.

agent_id.description: The agent that caused the error

task_id.description: The task that caused the error, if any

schema.description: Error details reported by the agent

post.description: Marks the agent as shutdown and offline, freeing any assigned tasks.

get.description: Returns an attack by id. This is used to get the details of an attack.
Returns the hash list for an attack.

get.summary: Request a new task from server
get.description: Request a new task from the server, if available.
Request the task information from the server.

post.summary: Submit a cracked hash result for a task
post.description: Submit a cracked hash result for a task.

post.summary: Submit a status update for a task
post.description: Submit a status update for a task. This includes the status of the current guess and the devices.

410.description: status received successfully, but task paused

schema.description: Hashcat status update for the task

post.description: Accept an offered task from the server.
Notify the server that the task is exhausted. This will mark the task as completed.
Abandon a task. This will mark the task as abandoned. Usually used when the client is unable to complete the task.

get.description: Gets the completed hashes for a task. This is a text file that should be added to the monitored directory to remove the hashes from the list during runtime.
Returns the configuration for the agent.

api_version.description: The minimum accepted version of the API

benchmarks_needed.description: Whether the agent needs to run benchmarks. False when the server already has valid benchmarks for this agent.

get.description: Authenticates the client. This is used to verify that the client is able to connect to the server.
